title: Please roll back pdf viewer
author: Vogelmensch
body: I'm just a regular user and no developer, but I rely on the Mega Android app and specifically the PDF viewer on a daily basis. The new user interface is annoying because the new buttons and the top bar block my view on the file. The way the PDF viewer was before was perfect. I don't know how other users see this, but I would love the old viewer back.

title: File/Folder picker permissions broken on Android 16
author: doyle31
body: ### Environment: - MEGA app Version(s): 16.2(260860537)(8482052cd3) - OS Version(s): LineageOS 23.2-20260325-NIGHTLY-akita (Android 16) - Devices/Simulators: Pixel ### Describe the bug When you pick a location to save files to, the file picker will say: Can't use this folder To protect your privacy, please use another folder ### Reproduction Steps 1. Go to the media tab 2. Click on a file 3. Click on the save button 4. The file/folder picker opens, but you are unable to select a folder to save stuff in ### Expected behavior The file picker should allow you to choose somewhere in `/sdcard` ### Screenshots / Logs ### Additional context `MANAGE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE` is not set in the manifest, is that why this is broken? `Internal storage/Download/MEGA Downloads` seems to be created by the MEGA app, but it is never used and the picker won't let you select it. This also might be helpful information:

title: Android 16 background play hangs randomly
author: benonymus
body:
Hey,

On android 16, I observed that the music playback in the background hangs after some time. It is not constant sometimes faster, sometimes slower and sometimes even restarts. I see it on a google pixel 9a and a Sony Xperia 1 mk6. 

I used the latest version from the play store. 

I tried to disable all optimizations for network and battery and still the same. The app is allowed to run in the background. 

If I reopen the app into the foreground the media resumes. 

To reproduce start a song in mega, and put the app in the background. Can lock the phone but not necessarily. After a bit of time the song will stop. As the time varies it is hard to say how long to wait but I never had to wait more than 1-2 mins sometimes even faster. 

Any idea why this happens?

title: When using the Android file picker to select a file from Mega, it gets stuck on "preparing" unless the file is marked as "offline"
author: eylenburg
body:
### Environment:
- MEGA app Version(s): 15.26.1
- OS Version(s): Android 16 (GrapheneOS)
- Devices/Simulators: Pixel 8

### Describe the bug
When using the Android file picker to select a file from Mega, it gets stuck on "preparing" unless the file is marked as "offline"

### Reproduction Steps
Add the different steps to reproduce the behavior.
1. Open a browser and go to a website where you can upload a file (e.g. online2pdf.com as an example)
2. The Android file manager opens. In the menu on the left you have the different apps that are integrated as a storage, e.g. you can select the Gallery app or the Mega app. Select Mega.
3. Pick a file from Mega that you want to upload to the website
4. It says "Preparing" with a spinning circle animation. But nothing ever happens.

### Expected behavior
File is temporarily downloaded from Mega and uploaded to the website. or more generally, a file is being opened directly from Mega without having to download/save it beforehand.

### Additional context
Tested with Firefox and Chrome browsers
If a file is marked as "offline" in the Mega app, then it works. But from the UI there's no indication that this is the case as all cloud files are shown and you can select any file from Mega. Also, it would be much more useful if you could select any file.

[Expose offline files via SAF](https://github.com/meganz/android/commit/144219e008c8b27dd018ac37112b005a8d624f01)

Maybe it's by design, but can it be extended to all files, or otherwise at least make it clear in the UI by e.g. greying out all non-offline files?

title: Extreme battery drain due to stuck sync
author: RomanBf
body: ### Environment: - MEGA app Version(s): 15.24 - OS Version(s): Android 16 - Devices/Simulators: Google Pixel 7 Pro ### Describe the bug Syncing a folder on my Android phone (Obsidian notes vault) with MEGA does not finish, and churns through CPU - draining the battery even if App is running in the background. I attached a screenshot from adb shell top command where you can see that it probably is in an infinite loop or something when interacting with the Android media module. The folder I am syncing does contain a hidden ".obsidian" folder and just some markdown files, could the hidden folder possibly be the reason? The same folder I also sync with MEGA sync Desktop app, where on the desktop MEGA sync App at least, I have to delete an automatically included sync exclusion rule when creating the sync so folders beginning with "." (dot) are synced. Maybe an edge case that is not properly tested when syncing that with Android too. From the desktop there is then a .megaignore file created too which gets auto-synced to Android. ### Reproduction Steps Add the different steps to reproduce the behavior. 1. Create an Obsidian vault and store it on Android internal storage -> Documents folder 2. Sync it to MEGA via Android App 3. Create a Mega sync Desktop sync to sync to PC 4. Configure the sync exclusions on desktop to allow folders and files beginning with dot "." to be synced 5. When changing a file in the vault, and MEGA Android syncs it, it stays in "Syncing" state and never finishes, while draining battery non stop (10-20% in one hour) of the phone. ### Expected behavior Sync should complete properly and no battery drain afterwards. ### Screenshots / Logs

title: [BUG] App shows "syncing" even if sync process has "finished" correctly.
author: GAC-Machine
body:
### Environment:

- MEGA app Version(s): `15.21(253110247)(09b5b0a0cf)`
- OS Version(s): `Android 15`
- Devices/Simulators: `Lenovo Tab M11`

### Describe the bug

App shows "`syncing`" status even if sync process has "`finished`" correctly.

### Reproduction Steps

Add the different steps to reproduce the behavior.
1. Set a sync in mega App (select local and remote folder to sync and sync method);
2. Add some files;
3. Let it sync;
4. On another device, do again step 1 (either a phone or a computer is fine);
6. Verify issue (even if both devices have same files (`device1 > mega_server > devices2` :=to have same files in both devices, mega server must be on the same version, up-to-date, so sync must finish with no error), status in first device is "`syncing`" not something like "`sync ended successfully`").
 
### Expected behavior

Show finished status either a in-app message or a notification. 

### Screenshots / Logs

See below screenshot to better understand what I'm referring to.

`mega_tablet_sync_message.png`

### Additional context

N/A

title: incorrect last modified time in downloaded files from sync
author: jiping-s
body:
### Environment:
- MEGA app Version(s): 15.20
- OS Version(s): 16
- Devices/Simulators: Samsung Galaxy S25 Enterprise

### Describe the bug
The last modified time of downloaded files by sync is always set to the current time.

### Reproduction Steps
Add the different steps to reproduce the behavior.
1. Add a sync to an empty local folder and a remote folder that contains files
2. Check the modification time of synced files

### Expected behavior
Timestamps of local files should be identical to that of files on MEGA website and PC.

### Screenshots / Logs
If applicable, add screenshots or logs to help explain your problem.

### Additional context
This makes the sync feature very unreliable - I don't know how it's tracking files exactly, but e.g. If I remove the sync and then add it back to the same local folder, it shows conflict errors on everything.

This does not happen to the windows client.

title: Insecure AES Usage: Defaults to ECB Mode
author: EhsanFir
body:
We are security researchers at Digit Institute in Germany. While reviewing [app/src/main/java/mega/privacy/android/app/utils/Util.java], we identified the following cryptographic issues:

At line 333, **Cipher.getInstance("AES")** is used.
This defaults to **"AES/ECB/PKCS5Padding"**, which is insecure because ECB mode leaks data patterns.
No initialization vector (IV) is used, which breaks semantic security and makes multiple encryptions of the same data vulnerable.
Recommendations:

Specify a secure mode, such as "AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding" or, preferably, use "AES/GCM/NoPadding" for authenticated encryption.
When using CBC mode, always generate a random IV per encryption, prepend it to the ciphertext, and use the same IV to initialize the cipher for decryption.
Addressing these issues will enhance the security of your cryptographic implementation.

title: Open https://mega.nz link by default in MEGA app
author: owocado
body:
### Describe the feature you'd like
Hello and greetings,

Currently MEGA app only opens these two links by default in app:
- www.mega.nz
- www.mega.co.nz

but more often than not, people on various social media share links in "https://mega.nz/..." format, which when clicked should ideally open in MEGA app but opens in default browser first then we have to click "Open in app" at the bottom to open in MEGA app. This tend to get tedious and frustrating if you're active MEGA user like me opening many mega.nz links everyday.

My request to you guys is to add "mega.nz" as valid deeplink for Android app so we users can open mega.nz links directly in MEGA app.

### Additional context
Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request.

title: Option to customize download location missing on Android 11+
author: abel1502
body:
### Environment:
- MEGA app Version(s): 15.4.1
- OS Version(s): Android 12
- Devices/Simulators: Samsung Galaxy M31

### Describe the bug
Apparently, the option to change the default download location is missing on devices with API version 30 and higher. This is clearly due to https://github.com/meganz/android/blob/master/app/src/main/res/xml-v30/preferences.xml not specifying the same `<androidx.preference.Preference>` `"settings_nested_download_location"` as https://github.com/meganz/android/blob/master/app/src/main/res/xml/preferences.xml does. I am unsure if this is intentional or not, but I believe it should be treated as a bug and fixed.

### Reproduction Steps
1. Go to Settings > Storage
2. See only "File management"

### Expected behavior
A "Download location" option in "Settings > Storage", which would allow changing the download location.
